title: PR description claims check-no-mobile-deps-at-root.cjs changes were NOT in the diff, but they are
body_excerpt:
The "Scope changes during execution" section states:

> `check-no-mobile-deps-at-root.cjs` — reverted by fix-locally — They are **NOT** in this PR's diff

But the diff clearly shows 6 new entries added to th
evidence: scripts/check-no-mobile-deps-at-root.cjs currently contains all 6 new entries in the FORBIDDEN list: @testing-library/react-native, hono, metro-config, metro-resolver, react, react-dom (lines 18, 26, 27, 28, 30, 31). The code changes are present and correct. The PR description discrepancy was a documentation-only issue in a now-merged PR — the live code is correct and the cleanup-plan.md in docs/_archive records the C5 P1 closure. No actionable code defect remains.

title: README.md and AGENTS.md reference bare db:generate (non-existent after rename to db:generate:dev)
body_excerpt: CLAUDE.md Sweep when you fix rule requires that when a drift exists at 3+ locations, the fix must sweep all current sites in the same PR (or document a tracked deferred sweep). The bare db:generate re
evidence: README.md line 77 reads `pnpm run db:generate:dev` and AGENTS.md line 167 reads `pnpm run db:generate:dev`. Both live onboarding documents were updated to use the renamed script. The bare `db:generate` reference no longer appears in either file.

title: Must-fix [1] GC6: delete-account.test.tsx:54 extends jest.mock("../hooks/use-account") without a gc1-allow annotation
body_excerpt: Must-fix [1] GC6: delete-account.test.tsx:54 extends jest.mock("../hooks/use-account") without a gc1-allow annotation. Add the annotation or refactor to jest.requireActual.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/delete-account.test.tsx line 57 has `// gc1-allow: screen tests mock account hooks to avoid full QueryClient/provider setup around the Expo Router page` annotation on the same line as the jest.mock call. The annotation is present and well-reasoned.

title: Must-fix [2] GRACE_PERIOD_MS unused in scheduleDeletion (deletion.ts:28)
body_excerpt: Must-fix [2] GRACE_PERIOD_MS unused in scheduleDeletion (deletion.ts:28). getDeletionStatus uses the constant; scheduleDeletion still has the inline expansion. The two can diverge. Use GRACE_PERIOD_MS in bo
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 12-13 define GRACE_PERIOD_DAYS and GRACE_PERIOD_MS. Both scheduleDeletion (line 24: `scheduledAt.getTime() + GRACE_PERIOD_MS`) and getDeletionStatus (line 125: `scheduledAt.getTime() + GRACE_PERIOD_MS`) now use the constant. No inline expansion remains.

title: Must-fix [3] .test-receipts/mobile.json records 3 focused --testMatch runs, not full suite
body_excerpt: Must-fix [3] .test-receipts/mobile.json records 3 focused --testMatch runs, not pnpm exec nx run mobile:test. The PR claims the full suite passed but the receipt does not show that. Regenerate with: bash s
evidence: The entire .test-receipts infrastructure was deliberately removed. Commit 8c4173a03 (`chore(test): remove mobile receipt gate`) deleted both scripts/record-test-receipt.sh and the .test-receipts directory. The PR template was updated to use `--findRelatedTests` instead. The receipt gate no longer exists, so the finding is moot.

title: Should-fix [4] canManageBilling with status===trial enables the store deep-link Manage section on iOS/Android
body_excerpt: Should-fix [4] canManageBilling with status===trial enables the store deep-link Manage section on iOS/Android for users with no native subscription. Test covers web only. Add a native-platform test or guard.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/subscription.tsx line 1192-1195: `canManageBilling = isPaidTier || hasActiveSubscription || (status === 'trial' && Platform.OS === 'web')`. Trial users only get the manage section on web, not on iOS/Android. Additionally, subscription.test.tsx line 1301-1333 has a native-platform test 'does not render native manage billing for server-side trial users' that sets Platform.OS to 'ios' and asserts manage-billing-button and manage-billing-web-info are absent.

title: Should-fix [5] getDeletionStatus returns scheduled:false when account row is missing; scheduleDeletion returns scheduledNow:true against non-existent account
body_excerpt: Should-fix [5] getDeletionStatus returns scheduled:false (not an error) when the account row is missing. scheduleDeletion then returns scheduledNow:true against a non-existent account. Add if (!row) throw aft
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 101-103: `if (!row) { throw new NotFoundError('Account'); }`. getDeletionStatus now throws NotFoundError when the row is missing. scheduleDeletion's tryScheduleDeletion returns null for a non-existent account (UPDATE affects 0 rows), then getDeletionStatus throws NotFoundError which propagates up, preventing a false scheduledNow:true return.

title: getDeletionStatus throws a plain Error, not a typed NotFoundError; plain Error causes 500 instead of 404
body_excerpt: `getDeletionStatus` throws a plain `Error`, not a typed `NotFoundError` (see inline comment on `deletion.ts:92`). The global `onError` handler converts `NotFoundError` → 404 cleanly; a plain `Error` fa
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 9: `import { ConflictError, NotFoundError } from '../errors'`. Line 102: `throw new NotFoundError('Account')`. The typed NotFoundError is used. The route handler at apps/api/src/routes/account.ts lines 34-38 checks `instanceof NotFoundError` and returns 404; all other errors are re-thrown to the global handler.

title: Blanket catch {} in GET /account/deletion-status maps every error to 404
body_excerpt:
**Blanket `catch {}` in `GET /account/deletion-status` maps every error to 404**
(inline comment on `apps/api/src/routes/account.ts` lines 30–33)

The bare `catch {}` swallows database errors, connection timeouts
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/account.ts lines 34-38: `if (!(err instanceof NotFoundError)) { throw err; }` — only NotFoundError produces a 404 response; all other errors are re-thrown for the global handler. The blanket catch-all behavior was fixed.

title: TOCTOU race in scheduleDeletion: two DB calls with a window for concurrent cancelDeletion to flip state
body_excerpt:
apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 41-46.

After the UPDATE returns empty (deletion already active), scheduleDeletion calls getDeletionStatus in a separate round-trip. In the narrow window between those two DB
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 19-44: scheduleDeletion now wraps tryScheduleDeletion + getDeletionStatus in a `for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 2; attempt += 1)` retry loop. If a concurrent cancelDeletion flips the state between the UPDATE and the subsequent read, the second attempt re-runs the UPDATE. After two failed retries it throws ConflictError with captureException, rather than crashing with a plain Error.

title: SQL WHERE uses strict > for cancellation guard; getDeletionStatus uses strict <; equal-timestamp edge case causes 500
body_excerpt:
apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 26 (SQL WHERE) vs line 88 (TypeScript getDeletionStatus).

The SQL WHERE uses strict > for the cancellation guard; getDeletionStatus uses strict <. If deletionCancelledAt
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 60: SQL uses strict `>` (`cancelledAt > scheduledAt`). Line 111: TypeScript uses `cancelledAt <= scheduledAt` meaning scheduled=true when equal. These are semantically consistent: equal timestamps → SQL does NOT update (cancelled=false for update guard), getDeletionStatus returns scheduled:true. A comment on lines 107-108 documents this alignment explicitly.

title: TOCTOU race in scheduleDeletion (duplicate finding - same as comment-4458661399)
body_excerpt:
`apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts` lines 41–46.

After the UPDATE returns empty (deletion already active), `scheduleDeletion` calls `getDeletionStatus` in a separate round-trip. In the narrow window between
evidence: Duplicate of comment-4458661399. apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 19-44: retry loop with 2 attempts wraps the UPDATE + read pattern. ConflictError thrown with captureException if both attempts fail.

title: SQL > vs TypeScript < comparison mismatch (duplicate finding - same as comment-4458661399 second finding)
body_excerpt:
`apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts` line 26 (SQL WHERE) vs line 88 (TypeScript `getDeletionStatus`).

The SQL WHERE uses strict `>` for the cancellation guard; `getDeletionStatus` uses strict `<`. If `deletionC
evidence: Duplicate of comment-4458661399. Current code: SQL line 60 uses strict `>`, TypeScript line 111 uses `<=` (scheduled=true when equal). Semantics are aligned and documented in comment on lines 107-108.

title: locallyScheduledRef guard silently neutered: effect's scheduled===true branch resets ref to false before stale refetch
body_excerpt: The ref is set to `true` synchronously inside `onConfirmDelete` to prevent the `useEffect` from bouncing the user back to `'initial'` when the status query hasn't caught up yet. But the effect's `sche
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/delete-account.tsx: locallyScheduledRef is set to true in onConfirmDelete (line 103) and reset to false ONLY in onCancelDeletion (line 123). The useEffect (lines 62-80) does NOT reset locallyScheduledRef at all — it checks locallyScheduledRef.current in the `scheduled===false && stage===scheduled` branch and returns early if true. The guard is intact.

title: GC6 deferral note missing from commit message when gc1-allow annotation added instead of removing mock
body_excerpt: CLAUDE.md GC6 says: *"Any time you edit a test file… scan it for `jest.mock('./…')` / `jest.mock('../…')` and remove the internal mocks."* This PR edited `delete-account.test.tsx` and added a `gc1-all
evidence: Commit 95548b1bc message includes `GC6-deferred: apps/mobile/src/app/delete-account.test.tsx (1 site - jest.mock('../hooks/use-account'))`. The deferral is recorded as required by CLAUDE.md. The gc1-allow annotation is present at line 57 of delete-account.test.tsx with a clear justification.

title: deletion.ts:50 uses >= for reschedule guard while isDeletionCancelled and getDeletionStatus use strict > / <
body_excerpt: `deletion.ts:50` uses `>=` to decide whether to allow a re-schedule, but both `isDeletionCancelled` (line 80) and `getDeletionStatus` (line 98) use strict `>` / `<`. If `deletionCancelledAt` and `delet
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 60: the SQL WHERE condition uses strict `>` (`sql`${accounts.deletionCancelledAt} > ${accounts.deletionScheduledAt}``), not `>=`. isDeletionCancelled (line 90) uses strict `>`. getDeletionStatus (line 111) uses `<=` which is semantically the complement. All three are consistent: equal timestamps are treated as 'not cancelled / still scheduled'.

title: getDeletionStatus throws with accountId in message when row is missing; Hono global handler may expose internal ID
body_excerpt: `getDeletionStatus` throws with the accountId in the message when the row is missing (deletion.ts lines 87–88). The new route handler has no try-catch, so if the account is removed between the auth-mid
evidence: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 102: `throw new NotFoundError('Account')` — no accountId in the message. The route handler at account.ts lines 31-39 wraps getDeletionStatus in try-catch, maps NotFoundError to a generic `{ code: 'NOT_FOUND', message: 'Account not found' }` 404 response, and re-throws other errors. No internal identifier is exposed.

title: useDeletionStatus has no explicit staleTime; scheduleDeletion terminal error is unclassified
body_excerpt: `useDeletionStatus` has no explicit `staleTime` (inline comment on `use-account.ts:57`). React Query defaults to staleTime 0, so every mount and app foreground fires a background refetch... `scheduleDele
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-account.ts line 65: `staleTime: 30_000` is present. apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 36-44: terminal error throws `new ConflictError('Account deletion scheduling conflict')` with `captureException` call including surface, reason, and accountId context. Both issues are resolved.

title: stage in useEffect dep array (delete-account.tsx line 71) causes effect re-run on every stage transition
body_excerpt:
**`stage` in `useEffect` dep array (`delete-account.tsx` line 71)**
The effect calls `setStage` and lists `stage` as a dependency, so every stage transition re-runs it. React's same-value bail-out keep
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/delete-account.tsx line 80: `}, [deletionStatus.data, setScreenStage]);` — `stage` is NOT in the dep array. The implementation reads stage via `stageRef.current` (line 63) instead of the `stage` state variable, which is the pattern recommended by the finding.
